The movement for higher wages
has begun in earnest. The firemen
on that portion of the Great North
ern known as the Spokane Falls 4
Northern railway have called upon
Il. ii. Butler to draw up their re-
quest, which will be presented to
Superintendent F. s. Forrest and
General Superintendent ii. A. Ker-
nedy of this city.


1t is possible that General Master
Mechanic Emerson will also be
present. The firemen request that
the scale on the following Great
Northern engines be increased to
$3.50 per 100 miles. or for 10 hours
work, and % cents per hour over.
time. This to apply to engines s,
10. II, -, BAG, 1929, 46S and All.


The firemen ask that the Spokane
Falls & Northern mogul engines
pay $2,40 for 100 miles, or 10 hours
and "a cents per hour overtime.


The larger the engine the more
cars hauled and consequently more
coal to be shoveled. A few of the
trunk lines are burning oil and have
made no reductions In their scale
of wages for firemen.


The firemen believe that condi
tions ere changing. prices higher in
every way and tonnage is being in,
creased to tho extent that a fire
mans lot is anything but an easy
one. The tiremen are confident that
MessrS. Forrest and Kennedy will
send their request to the general
office at st. Paul with their ap
| prevail.
